amendsScope: Presentation, navigation, theme, shared visible-state language, cross-product composition, and CSRF posture where spec 106 conflicts. Preserves predecessor domain/data hooks, routes unless consumer-traced, CSP, auth/privacy invariants, and certified business behavior.

superseded: Cards-local navigation where it conflicts with shared shell
fixed inventory where readiness-driven catalog differs

recertificationRouting.trigger: spec 106 successor behavior and owner-packet evidence complete
recertificationRouting.requiredRecord: bounded partial supersession of certified specs 092 and 100

evidenceLifecycleRole.flow: BUG-102 immutable acceptance evidence -> BUG-032 derived readiness snapshot -> spec 106 presentation

domainOwnership.workRoleHere: real browser routes and user journeys

workRouteOwnership.owner: spec 106 — SCOPE-106-09 owns leaf composition; SCOPE-106-02/05 own catalog registration and navigation cutover
workRouteOwnership.workGroup: route-free navigation group with null parent href; spec 106 owns its composition, label, and readiness-driven visibility as part of the shared shell (grounded in spec.md UI Scenario Matrix 'Work / Lists|Meals|Expenses', UC-106-010, and design.md Honest Integration Constraints)
workRouteOwnership.leaves: spec 106 owns the Lists, Meals/Recipes, and Expenses browser routes and user journeys composed over the existing owner domain APIs; those APIs and their owners are unchanged and are never reimplemented here
workRouteOwnership.supersessionOf100: The Work grouping is a readiness-driven navigation-catalog decision covered by the declared partial-supersession of spec 100's fixed assistant-first inventory (predecessorContractDisposition[100].superseded). Spec 100's sealed artifacts are not edited.
workRouteOwnership.noConflictWith092: Cards (spec 092 domain) is a separate top-level surface (SCOPE-106-10), not a Work leaf; there is no Work/Cards ownership conflict.
workRouteOwnership.mutationTrust: Every shell-reached Work mutation binds to BUG-070-001 MutationTrustGuard (server-validated session-bound anti-CSRF/Origin 403-before-mutation proof); spec 106 consumes that enforcement and does not implement it.

corpusOwnershipModel.identityModel: multiple authenticated identities with explicit roles/grants

executionHistory.summary: Created analyst-owned spec.md and state.json only. Existing runtime defects remain routed to bubbles.bug; no design, UX, scopes, report, user validation, source, tests, certification, commit, push, or deployment work was performed.

Authored UX-owned product IA, responsive shell, shared token/theme/state system, desktop/tablet/mobile wireframes, first-run and returning-use journeys, 16-row Card Management parity placement, accessibility contracts, exact failure behavior, routed design questions, and a stable real-stack Playwright scenario matrix. No design, scope, source, test, certification, commit, push, or deployment work was performed.

Created the contract-grade integration design for one readiness-driven product shell, source-locked theme/token/assets, coherent state and mutation presentation, unified-session consumption, packet-owned Search/Digest/Assistant/Graph/Recommendations/Cards/deployment composition, route compatibility, accessibility/responsive behavior, and real-stack Playwright architecture. All ten repair packets and spec 105 remain sole owners of their domain contracts. No spec, scope, report, user validation, scenario manifest, source, test, certification, status, commit, push, build, deployment, or CCManager change is claimed.

Reconciled the independent-review findings in analyst-owned requirements. Spec 106 partially supersedes certified 092/100 presentation/navigation/theme/CSRF decisions without editing them; adds one-login role matrices, global-corpus grants, CSRF/Origin, real Lists/Meals/Expenses browser journeys, BUG-083 domain ownership, BUG-102->BUG-032->106 evidence flow, off-traffic migration safety, test-only fault profiles, Search no-domain-work semantics, and accurate blocked spec-104 consumption. Preserved SCN-106-001..015 and added 016..022. No source, tests, design, scopes, report, user validation, certification, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Planning reconciliation (spec-scope-hardening harden phase) against the 2026-07-24T05:46 spec revision. Found artifact drift: spec.md/state.json at 05:46 defined 22 analyst scenarios (SCN-106-001..022) + 38 FRs + 11 NFRs, while scenario-manifest.json, scopes/_index.md, report.md, and planningProvenance counts were stuck at the 01:27 generation (15 scenarios). Reconciled: added SCN-106-016..022 to scenario-manifest.json mapped to existing scopes + already-planned tests (016->SCOPE-04 cross-family mutation canary; 017/018/019->SCOPE-09 Lists/Meals/Expenses UX-E2E-037/038/039; 020->SCOPE-15 UX-E2E-071 Admin Acceptance; 021->SCOPE-06 UX-E2E-027 blank-search; 022->SCOPE-12 UX-E2E-052 grant projection); raised specScenarioCount 15->22 and updated scenarioAuthority text in state.json + _index.md + report.md; resolved the named 'Work-route ownership' finding (106 owns the route-free Work navigation group plus the Lists/Meals/Expenses browser routes over existing owner domain APIs; the Work grouping is covered by the declared partial-supersession of spec 100's fixed inventory, NOT by editing 100; Cards/092 stays a separate surface); bound shell-reached Work mutations to BUG-070-001 MutationTrustGuard; recorded the BUG-102-001->BUG-032-004->106 one-way readiness/acceptance edge. Ran artifact-lint (exit 0) and traceability-guard (exit 0) for the first time on this packet and recorded them in report.md; no Test Plan row or DoD item count changed (144==144, G068 fidelity preserved). Residuals routed: design.md staleness (predates 05:46; does not name SCN-016..022 or the MutationTrustGuard term) -> bubbles.design; no design.md edit made. No source, authored test, test pass, runtime, browser, NFR measurement, certification, commit, or push is claimed; status set to in_progress (non-terminal) per the harden mandate.

F4 (independent-review LOW, advisory) design-side traceability polish: added the canonical 'MutationTrustGuard (AUTH-011)' citation to design.md prose. The Security And Privacy invariant describing how cookie-authenticated mutations keep their owning guard now names BUG-070-001's MutationTrustGuard (AUTH-011) as the owning same-origin + session-bound anti-CSRF guard each domain (for example BUG-083-002 Cards) binds to; the shell composes it and does not implement a parallel guard. This partially addresses the previously-routed design residual that design.md 'does not name the MutationTrustGuard term'. No substantive design change; scenarios, FRs, scopes, and Test Plan/DoD counts are unchanged. Guards artifact-lint.sh and traceability-guard.sh re-run before/after with identical PASS verdicts (exit 0); no regression. Read-only reference to BUG-070-001 design; BUG-070-001 not edited. No spec, scopes, scenario-manifest, test-plan, report, uservalidation, source, test, certification, status transition, commit, push, or deployment change is claimed.
